text.title: Kubernetes Production Readiness Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real kubernetes deployments, each paired with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.symptom: Re-rollouts pull a different image than the last verified release.
antipattern.root-cause: Workload pinned to image:latest or another floating tag.
antipattern.fix: Pin to immutable tag or sha256 digest; admission controller rejects floating tags in prod namespaces.

antipattern.symptom: Service receives traffic before warmup completes; cold-start errors visible to users.
antipattern.root-cause: Container missing readinessProbe; readiness defaults to true at pod start.
antipattern.fix: Add readinessProbe matching the warmup signal; verify ramp-up via canary.

antipattern.root-cause: Memory request < limit; kernel OOM-kills the largest delta when node is contended.
antipattern.fix: Set memory request = limit; alert on RSS approaching 90% of the limit.

antipattern.symptom: Node drain takes all replicas of a service down simultaneously.
antipattern.root-cause: Deployment has > 1 replicas but no PodDisruptionBudget.
antipattern.fix: Define a PDB with minAvailable ≥ 1; tune to PDB + replicas pair.
